meta_notes: stitch builder skipped (xlsx absent) — using July 10 02:17Z stitch JSON, ~31h old, inside the 48h window. english_only filter leaked 6 non-English items (Vietnamese OLAY launch coverage, 2.9M, excluded as claim-less event PR; Italian court story under guarantee thresholds). News scan ran 5 searches — nothing passed the ≤7d verified-dateline bar (SkinStylus ICYMI undateable; PLOS sunscreen-misinfo study ~3wk old): empty shelf is verified, not skipped. Run started 9:33 AM after the 6:30 cron — FULL quality, no cuts; manual publish required.

themes.corpus_position: If you have a mole that's not going away in six to eight weeks, if it's been changing, growing rapidly, bleeding — those are signs you should see a dermatologist.

angles.angle: Quiz, then teach the 6–8-week non-healing tell
Play ugly duckling: the mole that doesn't match is the worry
Add the answer beat quizzes skip — follows come from the teach

videos.author: drabby6
videos.caption: And don’t forget to exfoliate 1-2 times a week! Don’t use a retinoid when pregnant. What's your glass skin care routine? #glassskin #skincare #skincareroutine

videos.author: abby.waldmanmd
videos.caption: And don’t forget to exfoliate 1-2 times a week! Don’t use retinoids when pregnant. other retinoids like retinol or adapalene can be used instead of tretinoin Azelaic acid can be used at night instead

videos.author: skincarewithyuri
videos.caption: Filter routine💕✨ -olive oil & lemon shot everyday -eat collagen rich foods -find skincare product works on your skin -increase blood circulation in anyways! #GlowUp #glow #filter #flawlessskin

videos.author: skincarewithyuri
videos.caption: The best tasting collagen🍓🥰 [Gelatin mixture] -Combine 1/4 cup beef gelatin& 1/2 cup room temp water [Maple syrup mixture] - Combine 1 cup maple syrup (honey is ok too), 1/2 cup water and 2 tsp vanill

her_coverage.summary: she owns the lane this week: 1.2M TT (♥ gold, top-1% eng) + 1.2M IG in 2.7d; trifecta glass-skin routine 10d ago on both platforms

themes.corpus_position: The trifecta skincare for glass skin — azelaic acid, tretinoin, zinc-oxide barrier cream. And don't forget to exfoliate 1–2 times a week; don't use a retinoid when pregnant.

angles.angle: Film trifecta part 2 — series-tag it; magnets follow series
React to the olive-oil-and-lemon-shot 'filter routine' claim
Collagen you eat vs collagen you keep — gelatin-recipe react

themes.name: Sunscreen-substitute myths & sun-habit flexes
themes.receipt: 125× habit-flex monster (♥) + 1% ♥ diaper-cream myth-bust + a 15.9M civilian wave feeding it

videos.author: beautydermmd
videos.caption: Not her serum. Not her skincare. HER HABITS. 👀 Sun protection is elite behavior. UPF clothing. Wide-brim hat. Sunglasses. Shade. This is what dermatologists notice first. You should too. ☀️ #dermatolo

videos.author: drdrayzday
videos.caption: Diaper rash cream is not sunscreen. Always look for sunscreens that are labeled broad spectrum and with a stated SPF value (ideally 30 or higher). Don’t assume the presence of high concentrations of z

videos.author: drdrayzday
videos.caption: Diaper rash cream doesn’t not work as sunscreen. #sunscreen #diaperrashcream #sunprotection @rob.lea

her_coverage.summary: (IG) bemotrizinol filter explainer 307K, 8d ago; diaper-cream franchise: 2.4M + 1.8M TT stitches; hat/UPF position on record (TT, Oct 2025)

themes.corpus_position: Diaper creams with zinc oxide help almost any rash you can imagine — poison ivy or sunburn or perioral dermatitis or a rosacea flare. For sun: you want a broad-spectrum SPF 30 or higher.

angles.angle: Diaper cream on your rash, yes — instead of sunscreen, no
Celebrate the Hathaway flex: hat, UPF, shade — SPF is the floor
Myth-bust hormone-fear sunscreen claims with your bemotrizinol receipts

stitch_shortlist.author: @rob.lea1
stitch_shortlist.claim: Diaper rash cream + Bag Balm = 11-hour ocean sun protection

pitch.the_claim: An 11-hour strait swimmer survives the sun on zinc diaper cream and Bag Balm — so is diaper cream sunscreen?
pitch.why_it_stitches: 15.9M views pacing ~3.8M/day; drdrayzday's reply is already a 1% ♥ breakout (270K in 1.6d) — the myth has legs, and half-right claims are her best converters
pitch.your_fit: She IS the diaper-cream derm — 2.4M + 1.8M stitches on zinc-oxide barrier cream, trifecta caption 10d ago. Prior angle was barrier repair; the NEW angle is substitution: zinc oxide is in both, the broad-spectrum SPF rating isn't.
angles: 'You want me to WHAT?' — swim 11 hours on diaper cream
Half-right teach: zinc oxide yes, broad-spectrum SPF rating no
Extreme-use empathy: what actually survives open water
pitch.length: original eats ~5–10s; 30–45s total — this one earns the explainer beat

stitch_shortlist.author: @silent.tears0344
stitch_shortlist.claim: Anne Hathaway's covered-up beach moment — sun protection as a flex

pitch.the_claim: Anne Hathaway at the beach in full coverage — the internet is debating sun protection as a flex (confirm the clip in-app before drafting)
pitch.why_it_stitches: 6.7M on the civilian clip and the wave already minted derm breakouts (dr.derm.love 94× · dralice 7.2M) — the moment is proven derm-reactive
pitch.your_fit: Her position is on record: 'You still need broad-spectrum SPF, a hat, UPF clothing' (TT, Oct 2025) — and Mohs receipts make the celebration credible.
angles: Celebrate it: sun protection is elite behavior — keep the flex going
'She knows something you don't' — what Mohs surgeons see
Yes-and the derm wave: add the tinted-SPF melasma tip
pitch.length: original eats ~5s; 15–25s — celebration reacts stay short

stitch_shortlist.author: @dbeauty_glow
stitch_shortlist.claim: Influencers oil their skin to fake product results in ads

pitch.the_claim: That 'glow' in skincare ads is often just oil applied before filming
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1,264 comments on 60K likes — the comment section is doing the work; rising ~200K/day; deception reveals travel
pitch.your_fit: She's on record on influencer staging: 'I don't think most of these influencers are actually sleeping in all of these things — they put it on 30 minutes before filming' (morning-shed react, Sept 2025).
angles: 'Dermatologist spots the tricks' — 3 tells an ad faked the glow
Empathy-first: you're not failing — the before/after was staged
Show real skin texture on camera — what honest results look like

stitch_shortlist.author: @robertwblove
stitch_shortlist.claim: Avoid these sunscreens — they harm your hormones

pitch.the_claim: Chemical sunscreens are hormone disruptors — especially dangerous for children
pitch.why_it_stitches: 865 comments of confusion on 24K likes; the anti-sunscreen fear wave is the exact misinfo pattern derms are losing to — and she has the receipts
pitch.your_fit: Her bemotrizinol explainer 8d ago: 'newly approved by the FDA — used safely and effectively in European and Asian sunscreens for more than a decade.' Filter safety is her live lane; playbook backs broad-spectrum SPF 30+.
angles: Myth-bust the absorption-vs-harm distinction
Empathy-first for parents: the worry is real, the risk math isn't
Switch-don't-quit: worried about filters? go mineral — never bare
pitch.length: original eats ~8s; 45–60s — fear claims earn the full teach

stitch_shortlist.author: @heyitsmans
stitch_shortlist.claim: 'I'd rather get cancer the natural way than the chemical way'

pitch.the_claim: Sun-tanning is 'natural,' so its cancer risk beats sunscreen 'chemicals'
pitch.why_it_stitches: 2,715 comments on 48K likes — the hottest comment ratio in the feed, and the creator literally asks 'what do y'all think': an open door for a Mohs surgeon
pitch.your_fit: 'I remove skin cancers for a living — both non-melanoma skin cancers and melanoma' (her verbatim). Kim K tanning-bed react + her 10.2M UV-index hook = proven territory.
angles: Empathy-first: nobody chooses cancer — here's what 'natural' UV does
Bring the Mohs receipts: what a removal actually looks like
Her UV-index framing: 'let me convince you not to'

stitch_shortlist.author: @skinecologic
stitch_shortlist.claim: '10% azelaic acid' products often aren't 10% active

pitch.the_claim: The azelaic-acid percentage on the label isn't the concentration your skin actually gets
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1,202 comments on 22K likes — confusion begging for an expert; azelaic is having a moment and it's HER ingredient
pitch.your_fit: 'I use azelaic acid every single day for my redness and rosacea' (Feb 2026 beginners guide, verbatim) — and azelaic anchors her glass-skin trifecta from 10d ago. Yes-and with her prescriber context.
angles: Yes-and: formulation matters — the prescription difference
Beginner's map: who actually benefits from azelaic
Tie it to the trifecta: why azelaic anchors her glass-skin routine
